findings.title: Ingest metric globals are used before this package guarantees initialization
findings.why: This looks like a hidden bootstrap dependency: `ingestcli` now observes and reads `ingestmetric` globals that stay nil unless some other package imports `pkg/metrics` and runs `InitMetrics()` first.
findings.risk_if_unchanged: The new duration tests can panic before they validate anything, and any future direct caller of `pkg/ingestor/ingestcli` that does not also pull in `pkg/metrics` will inherit the same nil-observer runtime failure.
findings.evidence: `WriteIngestAPIDuration`, `WriteAPIDuration`, and `IngestAPIDuration` are declared as nil package globals in `pkg/ingestor/ingestmetric/metric.go` and are assigned only by `InitIngestMetrics()`. The changed tests read `ingestmetric.WriteIngestAPIDuration` directly at lines 115 and 185 without importing `pkg/metrics`, while `startChunkedHTTPRequest` now unconditionally calls `ingestmetric.WriteAPIDuration.Observe(...)` on line 125.
findings.change_request: Please add an explicit initialization path for these collectors where they are used, or make the metric package safe by default with a private `sync.Once` initializer or no-op observer fallback. `add UT for it` with a direct `ingestcli` path that does not rely on `pkg/metrics` side effects.
